cards.front: What is a copy constructor in C++?
cards.back: A copy constructor is a special member function that creates a copy of an object.

cards.front: When do you need to define a copy constructor?
cards.back: You need to define a copy constructor when you want to create a copy of an object and pass it to another function or return it.

cards.front: What is the syntax of a copy constructor in C++?
cards.back: The syntax of a copy constructor in C++ is: MyClass(const MyClass &obj);

cards.front: What happens when you use the copy constructor with auto keyword?
cards.back: When you use the copy constructor with the auto keyword, it creates a temporary object that is a copy of the original object.

cards.front: Can you define a copy constructor for a primitive data type in C++?
cards.back: No, you cannot define a copy constructor for a primitive data type in C++.
